team_news: Ivory Coast are framed as an athletic, fast-paced side under Emerse Fae, with Franck Kessie a key midfield reference.
Ecuador under Sebastian Beccacece are described as more defensive and grinding, built around Moises Caicedo, with Enner Valencia and Kendry Paez as attacking outlets.
No confirmed matchday lineups or late suspensions were verified.
weather_venue: Philadelphia Stadium/Lincoln Financial Field is an outdoor venue; exact kickoff weather was not verified.
Tournament-wide weather reporting flags heat, humidity and thunderstorms as risks in central/eastern North America.
historical_structural: Ivory Coast return to the World Cup after missing recent editions and carry AFCON-era momentum.
Ecuador have recent World Cup continuity and a defensive identity, but limited scoring margin.
Group E contains a heavy favorite in Germany, so this match is structurally important for second/third-place positioning.

risks: Verified Sporttery prices and official market status unavailable.
Outdoor weather and storm risk were not pinned to exact kickoff conditions.
One early goal could flip the match from low-total to transition-heavy.
Both teams have enough physicality for cards/set pieces to become decisive.

post_match_review.variance_note: Low-goals lean was right and 1-0 was a listed candidate; the decisive goal arrived late after Ecuador failed to convert its chances.
